quiz.title: Midweek Quiz: Units 1 & 2
quiz.description:
This quiz covers material from **Units 1 and 2** (Days 1–5): ocean geography, seawater chemistry, phytoplankton and primary production, and zooplankton and food webs.
You have **30 minutes** and **2 attempts** — your highest score will be kept.

questions.question: The Southern Ocean is sometimes considered Earth's fifth ocean basin.

Which process drives seafloor spreading at mid-ocean ridges? Magma upwelling from the mantle creating new oceanic crust Subduction of oceanic crust beneath continental crust Erosion of continental shelf sediments Glacial isostatic rebound
Ocean acidification occurs primarily because the ocean absorbs excess atmospheric: Carbon dioxide (CO₂) Nitrogen (N₂) Methane (CH₄) Sulfur dioxide (SO₂)
Phytoplankton produce roughly 50% of Earth's oxygen through photosynthesis.

Which factor most directly triggers the spring phytoplankton bloom in temperate seas? Increased daylight hours combined with shallow mixed-layer depth allowing nutrient-rich water near the surface A sudden increase in ocean temperature Upwelling driven by seasonal winds Migration of zooplankton away from surface waters

The "biological pump" refers to: The transport of carbon from the surface ocean to the deep sea through sinking organic matter Mechanical mixing of ocean layers by wind Upwelling of deep, nutrient-rich water to the surface Filtration of seawater by whale sharks and manta rays
Copepods are considered the most [abundant] animals on Earth and serve as a critical link between [phytoplankton] and higher trophic levels.
Explain how thermohaline circulation (the "ocean conveyor belt") is driven by differences in water density. In your answer, identify at least two factors that affect seawater density.
